reviews.review_msg: His lecture slides were very organized and well-made. His lectures were easy to understand, and he didnt just read off the slides, he actually explained the content beyond what was in the slides, which is why going to lectures is very helpful. Exams were fairly made, but the grading is very very tough, so you gotta really know ur stuff
its like they fail you purposely so you're forced to retake it so the school gets more money from you

I LOVED this course at the start with Nadine but when Claydon took over, I have grown to hate this course. The way Claydon and the TA, Lara, mark is ridiculous. They do not encourage students to learn and search for ways for students to fail. Its like they have fun finding ways to not give students full marks for nearly perfect answers.
doesn't care about students and horrible at explaining topics

I loved the beginning of this course despite how difficult it was but I have come to hate it due to Claydon switching in as our professor. His lectures are so bland and his tests are made to trick students. I went to talk to him about a question I felt was unfair and he just smirked and was happy he tricked students...  #Bring Nadine back

The first half of this class I felt so much more engaged with the content (shoutout to Nadine), however I cannot say the same for the second half of the course with Claydon. I felt like the marking was unfair and there were questions to trick you despite him saying he wouldn't do that. His teaching is also dry and he doesn't answer questions well.

Dr Claydon was one of the worst professors I have had. His lectures are bland and he bascially just reads off the slides. His tests are unfair with questions to try and trick students and when I talked to him about how one of the questions was unfair he basically laughed in my face. Doesn't care about his students at all

I found that he sometimes did not know some of his slides properly which made it really hard to understand what he was saying. On top of that the exams were super complex because he wanted you to be super detailed on 50 min midterm exams.

Expects you to regurgitate the entire course onto the midterm in large essay style paragraphs. 45% class average on MT2 and he told the class he was disappointed and we should just try harder lol. Said that we needed to be able to describe the material at length but his lectures are power point diagrams with sparse notes. The dichotomy!

Thomas Claydon doesn't care about his students at all.  You need to remember every word on every lecture slide or you won't do well on the exams. The second midterm had a failing grade and he blamed the class for the poor grade instead of reflecting on his poor teaching ability and unfair exams. would highly recommend choosing another professor.
This is the worst professor ever. He does not care about students nor about their learning. Please do not take this class if you want to remain sane.

This was a very hard course for me. If you have ADHD good luck, lecture slides make no sense if you do not obtain every detail that he says in lecture, so get ready to type, you will miss things. The worst part of the class is the grading scheme. If you do not write 4 essays with the exact words as the answers you do bad. Midterm 2 avg = a fail!

I give Tom a 5 due to his ability to explain difficult concepts well and his willingness to help outside of class. However, you must take notes or record lectures due to his bare bones lecture slides. His choice of a TA is poor; the decision of cutting down the time on the final with no notice was unfair. He made sure the average was B regardless.

Don't listen to bots saying "he genuinely wants his students to succeed" - the average for midterm 2 was 56% and the final 50%. He was genuinely indifferent to peoples struggles during covid; refused to record lectures during a *pandemic* and didn't tell us the format for the final until literally the time of the final. + no textbook & bad slides.

Tom is a really good prof. He is very good at explaining the concepts. However, his lecture sides are not very detailed so if you miss a couple of classes chances are you will be completely clueless. His exams are essay style and each question is worth a lot of marks. So you have to know the material well to do well in a limited amount of time.
Great prof, taught a month of 412. Very clear in lectures and broke down complex material well. Wish I had him for 305.

Tom is a really good prof. If you're gonna take 305 I would defs take it with him vs any other prof. The material isn't easy but he tells you exactly what you need to know. The exams are fair and fairly predictable.

Tom was an AMAZING prof! You knew exactly what you would be tested on and the midterms and final should have been a breeze if you put the time and effort into the class. Hands-down best prof I've had at SFU.

Amazing prof. Great at explaining concepts and will go out of his way to find answers to your questions. Exams consist of essay questions only. The questions on midterm 1, 3, and the final were somewhat predictable, but midterm 2 has random questions that no one was expecting. Overall fair exams and marking.

He has super powers when it comes to explaining concepts and he's one of the very few profs that can maintain my full attention throughout the entire lecture. His questions are straightforward but you have to include every detail to get the full marks which in the context of what he teaches is fair.

*Cue Lennon's "Imagine"* Can you imagine if all the profs at SFU could teach like Claydon? Wow. Tution payments might actually be worth it.  The guy has crystal clear notes and actually answers questions people ask in class. Plus, he has a British accent, so it feels like you're studying at Oxford or something. Sign this guy to an extension.
The best prof I've had so far at SFU! He's very helpful during office hours and has great notes! Love him!
Absolutely amazing prof.  His notes were very clear and he was also willing to help.
Really hard class with a really hard professor. His notes are organized by he tests on every little details. Make sure you ready and understand and memorized everything on the notes.
Loved him, great prof!
